text: The double life of Alfred Bloggs
zh: 阿尔弗雷德·布洛格斯的双重生活

text: Why did Alf want a white-collar job?
zh: 阿尔夫为什么想要一份白领工作？

text: These days, people who do manual work often receive far more money than people who work in offices.
zh: 如今，从事体力劳动的人往往比坐办公室的人挣得多得多。

text: People who work in offices are frequently referred to as 'white-collar workers' for the simple reason that they usually wear a collar and tie to go to work.
zh: 在办公室工作的人常被称为“白领”，原因很简单：他们上班时通常要穿衬衫、打领带。

text: Such is human nature, that a great many people are often willing to sacrifice higher pay for the privilege of becoming white-collar workers.
zh: 人性就是如此，许多人往往宁愿牺牲更高的薪水，也要享有成为白领的那份体面。

text: This can give rise to curious situations, as it did in the case of Alfred Bloggs who worked as a dustman for the Ellesmere Corporation.
zh: 这就可能引发一些奇怪的状况，阿尔弗雷德·布洛格斯的例子便是如此——他在埃尔斯米尔公司当清洁工。

text: When he got married, Alf was too embarrassed to say anything to his wife about his job.
zh: 结婚时，阿尔夫因为太难为情，一直没告诉妻子自己的工作是什么。

text: He simply told her that he worked for the Corporation.
zh: 他只是告诉她，自己在那家公司上班。

text: Every morning, he left home dressed in a smart black suit.
zh: 每天早上，他都穿着一身笔挺的黑西装出门。

text: He then changed into overalls and spent the next eight hours as a dustman.
zh: 然后他换上工作服，接下来八个小时都在做清洁工的工作。

text: Before returning home at night, he took a shower and changed back into his suit.
zh: 晚上回家前，他会先洗个澡，再换回西装。

text: Alf did this for over two years and his fellow dustmen kept his secret.
zh: 阿尔夫这样过了两年多，他的清洁工同事们都替他保守着这个秘密。

text: Alf's wife has never discovered that she married a dustman and she never will, for Alf has just found another job.
zh: 阿尔夫的妻子从未发现自己嫁给了一个清洁工，而且她永远也不会知道了，因为阿尔夫刚刚找到了另一份工作。

text: He will soon be working in an office.
zh: 他很快就要去办公室上班了。

text: He will be earning only half as much as he used to, but he feels that his rise in status is well worth the loss of money.
zh: 他的收入将只有原来的一半，但他觉得地位的提升完全值得这点收入上的损失。

text: From now on, he will wear a suit all day and others will call him 'Mr.Bloggs', not 'Alf'.
zh: 从此以后，他将整天穿着西装，别人也会称他为“布洛格斯先生”，而不再叫他“阿尔夫”。
